1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, called by God's will to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and our brother Sosthenes.

To the church of God in Corinth — to those who have been made holy in Christ Jesus and called to be his holy people — together with all everywhere who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
I always thank my God for you, because of the grace God has given you in Christ Jesus.
In him you have been made rich in every way, in all your speaking and all your knowledge,
because what we told you about Christ was proven true in you.
So you do not lack any spiritual gift as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to appear.
He will keep you strong to the end, so that you will be without blame on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
God is faithful. He is the One who called you to share life with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.

Brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to agree with one another, so that there are no divisions among you, and so that you are joined together in the same mind and purpose.
My brothers and sisters, some from Chloe's household have told me that there is quarreling among you.
What I mean is this: one of you says, I follow Paul; another, I follow Apollos; another, I follow Peter; and another, I follow Christ.
Is Christ divided? Was Paul nailed to the cross for you? Were you baptized in the name of Paul?
I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius,
so that no one can say you were baptized in my name.
(Yes, I also baptized the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not remember baptizing anyone else.)
For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the good news — and not with clever words, so that the cross of Christ would not lose its power.
The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being lost, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
For the Scripture says: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise; I will set aside the cleverness of the clever.
Where is the wise person? Where is the teacher of the law? Where is the great debater of this age? Has God not shown that the world's wisdom is foolish?
For in his wisdom, God made sure that the world could not know him through its own wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the “foolish” message we preach.
Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom,
but we preach Christ nailed to the cross. This is a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to other nations,
but to those whom God has called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
For the “foolishness” of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the “weakness” of God is stronger than human strength.
Brothers and sisters, think about what you were when God called you. Not many of you were wise by human standards; not many were powerful; not many were of noble birth.
But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise. God chose the weak things of the world to shame the strong.
God chose the lowly things, the things looked down on, and the things that are nothing, to bring to nothing the things that are something,
so that no one can brag in God's presence.
It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus, who has become for us wisdom from God — that is, our right standing with God, our holiness, and our freedom.
So, as the Scripture says: Whoever wants to brag should brag about the Lord.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, when I came to you, I did not come with fancy words or great wisdom as I told you about God.
For I decided to know nothing while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and him nailed to the cross.
I came to you weak, afraid, and trembling.
My message and my preaching did not depend on clever, persuasive words. Instead, they came with the proof of the Spirit's power,
so that your faith would not rest on human wisdom, but on God's power.
Yet among those who are mature, we do speak a wisdom — but not the wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age, who are coming to nothing.
Instead, we speak God's hidden wisdom, a wisdom that was a secret. God planned it before time began, for our glory.
None of the rulers of this age understood it. If they had, they would not have nailed the Lord of glory to the cross.
But as the Scripture says: No eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no mind has imagined what God has prepared for those who love him.
But God has shown these things to us through his Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, even the deep things of God.
For who knows a person's thoughts except that person's own spirit within him? In the same way, no one knows the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God.
We have not received the spirit of the world, but the Spirit who is from God, so that we may understand what God has freely given us.
This is what we speak about — not in words taught by human wisdom, but in words taught by the Spirit, explaining spiritual things to spiritual people.

The person without the Spirit does not accept the things that come from the Spirit of God. They are foolishness to him, and he cannot understand them, because they are understood only through the Spirit.
The person who has the Spirit can judge all things, but he himself is not subject to anyone's judgment.
For: Who has known the mind of the Lord, so as to instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, I could not speak to you as spiritual people, but as people still ruled by their sinful nature — as babies in Christ.
I gave you milk, not solid food, because you were not ready for it. And you are still not ready,
because you are still ruled by your sinful nature. There is jealousy and quarreling among you. Does that not show that you are ruled by your nature and acting like ordinary people?
For when one of you says, I follow Paul, and another, I follow Apollos, are you not acting like ordinary people?
After all, who is Apollos? And who is Paul? We are only servants through whom you believed — each doing the work the Lord gave us.
I planted the seed, Apollos watered it, but God made it grow.
So neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything; only God matters, the One who makes things grow.
The one who plants and the one who waters work toward the same goal, and each will be rewarded for his own work.
For we are God's fellow workers; you are God's field, God's building.
By the grace God gave me, I laid a foundation like a wise builder, and someone else is building on it. But each one should be careful how he builds.
For no one can lay any foundation other than the one already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
If anyone builds on this foundation using gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or straw,
their work will be shown for what it is. The Day will make it clear, because it will be revealed by fire. The fire will test the quality of each person's work.
If what someone has built survives, that person will receive a reward.
If it burns up, that person will suffer loss. He himself will still be saved, but only as one escaping through the flames.
Don't you know that you yourselves are God's temple, and that God's Spirit lives in you?
If anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy him. For God's temple is holy, and you are that temple.
Do not fool yourselves. If any of you thinks he is wise by the standards of this age, he should become a “fool” so that he may become truly wise.
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness to God. As the Scripture says: He catches the wise in their own cleverness.
And again: The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are worthless.
So no one should brag about human leaders! Everything belongs to you,
whether Paul or Apollos or Peter, or the world or life or death, or the present or the future — all are yours,
and you are Christ's, and Christ is God's.
You should think of us as servants of Christ, the ones God has trusted to do the work of making known God’s secret truths.
Someone who does this work of God must always be faithful and do what God wants him to do.
But it is not important to me what people or a judge thinks of my work. I don’t even try to decide myself if I have done the right thing.
I don’t know of anything that I have done wrong, but I don’t say that I am innocent. God will say if I am guilty or not.

That is why you must not judge anyone now. The time for judging will be when the Lord comes. He will shine light on everything that is now hidden in darkness. He will make known the secret purposes of our hearts. Then the praise each person should get will come from God.

Brothers and sisters, I have used Apollos and myself as examples for you. I did this so that you could learn from us the meaning of the words, Follow what the Scriptures say. No one must say that one person is more important than another person.
Why do you think you are better than someone else? Everything that you have, God gave you. Why do you boast about the things that you did not work for?
You think you have everything you need. You think you are rich. You think you have become kings without us. I wish you really were kings. Then we could rule together with you

We are apostles, but I think God made us unimportant and He gave us the last place of all people. We are like prisoners on our way to die in a place where everyone can see us. And now everyone in the world, angels and people, can see how we die.

Everyone thinks that we, the apostles, are fools because we work for Christ. but you think you are so wise in Christ. You think we are weak but you are strong. You think you are important and we are not important.
Even now we still don’t have enough to eat or drink, and we don’t have enough clothes. We often get beatings. We have no homes.
We work very hard for food and clothes for ourselves. When people curse us, we pray that God will bless them. When they do bad things to us, we keep quiet and hold out.
When they say bad things about us, we try to say something good that will help them. We have become like the worst people in the world, like dirt. We are like dirty things that people throw away.
I am not trying to make you feel ashamed, but I am writing this to warn you as my own children.

You have ten thousand teachers to guide you in Christ, but you do not have many fathers who led you to Christ and assumed responsibility for you, for I became your father in Christ Jesus through the good news of salvation.
That is why I beg you to live as I live.

That is why I sent Timothy to you. I have helped Timothy to know the Lord Jesus and now he is like a son to me, and I love him very much and Timothy is faithful. He will help you to remember how I live in Christ Jesus. The way of life that I teach in every meeting of the church wherever I am.
Some of you thought I would never come to you again and they think they are very important now.
But I will soon come to you, if God wants me to come. I will find out if these people only talk or if they also do something.
Because where God is King, people don’t only talk about the things that God wants. They also do them with the power that God gives them.
You can choose what I must do when I come to you: Must I be angry, or must I be loving and friendly?

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: It is actually reported that there is sexual sin among you, and a kind that even the people who do not know God do not allow: a man is sleeping with his father's wife.
And you are proud! Should you not rather be filled with grief and remove from your group the man who did this?
Even though I am not there with you in body, I am with you in spirit. And I have already judged the man who did this, just as if I were there.
So when you come together in the name of our Lord Jesus, and I am with you in spirit, and the power of our Lord Jesus is present,
hand this man over to Satan, so that his sinful nature may be destroyed and his spirit saved on the day of the Lord.
Your bragging is not good. Don't you know that a little yeast spreads through the whole batch of dough?
Get rid of the old yeast, so that you may be a new batch without yeast — as you really are. For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
So let us keep the feast, not with the old yeast of meanness and evil, but with the bread without yeast — the bread of honesty and truth.
I wrote to you in my letter not to keep company with people who are sexually immoral.
I did not mean the people of this world who are immoral, or greedy, or cheaters, or who worship idols. If that were so, you would have to leave the world.

But now I am writing that you should not keep company with anyone who calls himself a believer but is sexually immoral, or greedy, or worships idols, or insults others, or gets drunk, or cheats people. Do not even eat with such a person.
What business is it of mine to judge those outside the church? But you are to judge those inside, are you not?
God will judge those outside. So, as the Scripture says: Remove the evil person from among you.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When one of you has a problem with another believer, why do you go to the law courts and let people who are not right with God decide it? Why not let God's people decide?
Don't you know that God's people will one day judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, are you not able to decide small things?
Don't you know that we will judge angels? So of course we can decide about the things of this life.
So when you have these everyday problems, why do you take them to judges who mean nothing to the church?
I say this to make you ashamed. Is there really no one among you wise enough to settle a problem between two believers?
Instead, one believer takes another to court — and does it in front of people who do not believe!
The truth is, going to law against each other means you have already lost. Why not just accept the wrong? Why not let yourself be cheated?
But instead you do wrong and cheat others — and you do this to your own brothers and sisters.

Don't you know that people who do wrong will have no part in God's kingdom? Do not be fooled. People who sin sexually, or worship idols, or are unfaithful in marriage, or men who have sexual relations with other men,
or who steal, or are greedy, or get drunk, or say evil things about others, or cheat people — none of them will have a part in God's kingdom.
Some of you were like that. But you were washed clean. You were made holy. You were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
Someone may say: I am allowed to do anything. But not everything is good for you. I am allowed to do anything, but I will not let anything control me.
Food is for the stomach, and the stomach is for food — but God will put an end to both. The body is not for sexual sin. The body is for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
By his power God raised the Lord from death, and he will raise us up too.
Don't you know that your bodies are parts of Christ himself? So should I take a part of Christ and join it to a prostitute? Never!
Don't you know that a man who joins himself to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For God says: The two will become one flesh.
But the one who joins himself to the Lord becomes one with him in spirit.
Run away from sexual sin. Every other sin a person does is outside the body, but the one who sins sexually sins against his own body.
Don't you know that your body is a temple for the Holy Spirit? The Spirit lives in you, and you received him from God. You do not belong to yourself.
God bought you for a price. So use your body to bring honor to God.

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now about the things you wrote to me: It is good for a man not to have sexual relations with a woman.
But because there is so much sexual sin, each man should have his own wife, and each woman her own husband.
The husband should give his wife what he owes her, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
The wife does not have full rights over her own body; her husband does. In the same way, the husband does not have full rights over his own body; his wife does.
Do not keep apart from each other, except by agreement for a time, so that you can give yourselves to prayer. Then come together again, so that Satan will not tempt you because you lack self-control.
I say this as a suggestion, not as a command.
I wish everyone were like me. But each person has his own gift from God; one has this gift, another has that.
To those who are not married, and to widows, I say: It is good for them to stay unmarried, as I am.
But if they cannot control themselves, they should marry, because it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
To the married I give this command (not I, but the Lord): A wife must not leave her husband.
But if she does, she must stay unmarried or else make peace with her husband. And a husband must not divorce his wife.
To the rest I say this (I, not the Lord): If a believing man has a wife who does not believe, and she is willing to live with him, he must not divorce her.
And if a believing woman has a husband who does not believe, and he is willing to live with her, she must not divorce him.
For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through her believing husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but as it is, they are holy.
But if the one who does not believe wants to leave, let him leave. In such cases the believing brother or sister is not bound. God has called us to live in peace.
After all, wife, how do you know that you will save your husband? And husband, how do you know that you will save your wife?
Each person should live the life the Lord has given him, the life he was in when God called him. This is the rule I give in all the churches.
Was a man already circumcised when he was called? He should not try to undo it. Was a man uncircumcised when he was called? He should not get circumcised.
Being circumcised means nothing, and being uncircumcised means nothing. What matters is obeying God's commands.
Each person should stay in the situation he was in when God called him.
Were you a slave when you were called? Do not let it trouble you. But if you can gain your freedom, take the chance.
For the one who was a slave when the Lord called him is now the Lord's free person. And the one who was free when he was called is now Christ's slave.
You were bought for a price; do not become slaves of other people.
Brothers and sisters, each person should stay with God in the situation he was in when God called him.
Now about those who have never married: I have no command from the Lord, but I give my opinion as one whom the Lord, in his mercy, has made trustworthy.
Because of the troubles of the present time, I think it is good for a person to stay as he is.
Are you married? Do not seek a divorce. Are you unmarried? Do not look for a wife.
But if you do marry, you have not sinned. And if a young woman marries, she has not sinned. But those who marry will face many troubles in this life, and I want to spare you that.
What I mean, brothers and sisters, is that the time is short. From now on, those who have wives should live as if they did not;
those who mourn, as if they did not; those who are happy, as if they were not; those who buy something, as if it were not theirs to keep;
and those who use the things of the world, as if not depending on them. For this world as we know it is passing away.
I want you to be free from worry. An unmarried man cares about the Lord's work and how to please the Lord.
But a married man cares about the things of this world and how to please his wife.

So his interests are divided. An unmarried woman or a young woman cares about the Lord's work, so that she may be holy in both body and spirit. But a married woman cares about the things of this world and how to please her husband.
I am saying this for your own good, not to hold you back, but so that you may live in a right way and give yourselves fully to the Lord without distraction.
If anyone feels he is not acting properly toward the young woman he is engaged to, and if his desires are strong and the time has come, he should do as he wishes; it is not a sin. They should marry.
But the man who has settled it in his own heart, who is under no pressure but has control over his own will, and who has decided not to marry her, also does well.
So the one who marries the young woman does well, and the one who does not marry her does even better.
A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry anyone she wishes, but only in the Lord.
In my opinion, she is happier if she stays as she is. And I think that I too have the Spirit of God.

Now I will write about meat that is sacrificed to idols. It is certainly true that we all have knowledge. But this knowledge only fills people with pride. It is love that helps the church grow stronger.
Those who think they know something do not yet know anything as they should.
But whoever loves God is known by God.
Now I want to talk about meat that has been sacrificed to idols. We all know that no idol in the world is alive. We know that there is only one true living God.
Many people say there are gods in heaven or on earth and we know that people worship many gods and say they are lords

For us there is only one God, and he is our Father. All things came from the Father, and we live for him. And there is only one Lord, Jesus Christ. All things were made through him, and we also have life through him.

But not all the believers understand that there is only one God. They served these idols for many years before they became believers. They think that when they eat meat that was sacrificed to idols, they serve those idols. And because these believers feel guilty very easily, they think that they have sinned.
But food will not bring us closer to God. Refusing to eat does not make us less pleasing to God, and eating does not make us closer to him.
Those of you who think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols must be careful because other believers may sin when they see what you eat.

If you think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols and someone who does not have a strong faith sees you eating in a temple of an idol, what will he think? He will think that he too can eat there and maybe he will then start to serve those idols.
His faith is not strong and because you think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols, he may be lost. But he is a believer, and Christ died for him.
When you do something that would make other believers feel guilty, then you sin against Christ.
That is why, if food makes someone sin, and that person believes in Christ like I do, then I will never eat meat again so that I will not make other believers sin.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not the result of my work for the Lord?
Even if others do not accept me as an apostle, surely you do, for you yourselves are the proof that I am an apostle in the Lord.
This is my answer to those who question me.
Do we not have the right to food and drink?
Do we not have the right to take along a believing wife, as the other apostles do, and the Lord's brothers, and Peter?
Or are Barnabas and I the only ones who must work to support ourselves?
Who serves as a soldier and pays his own way? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its grapes? Who takes care of a flock and does not drink its milk?
Am I only saying this from a human point of view? Does the law not say the same thing?
For it is written in the law of Moses: Do not stop the ox from eating while it treads out the grain. Is God only concerned about oxen?
Surely he says this for us, doesn't he? Yes, it was written for us, because the one who plows and the one who threshes should be able to work in the hope of sharing in the harvest.
If we have planted spiritual seed among you, is it too much for us to receive some material help from you?
If others have this right to be supported by you, do we not have it even more? But we have not used this right. Instead, we put up with anything rather than get in the way of the good news of Christ.
Don't you know that those who work in the temple get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is offered there?
In the same way, the Lord has commanded that those who preach the good news should make their living from it.
But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing this so that something will be done for me. I would rather die than let anyone take away this thing I am proud of.
For when I preach the good news, I cannot brag, because I am required to do it. How terrible it would be for me if I did not preach the good news!
If I preach because I choose to, I have a reward. But even if I do not choose it, I have still been trusted with this job.
So what is my reward? Just this: that when I preach the good news, I can offer it free of charge, and so not make full use of my rights as a preacher.
Though I am free and belong to no one, I have made myself a slave to everyone, to win as many as possible.
To the Jews I became like a Jew, to win the Jews. To those under the law I became like one under the law (though I myself am not under the law), to win those under the law.
To those without the law I became like one without the law (though I am not free from God's law, but am under Christ's law), to win those without the law.
To the weak I became weak, to win the weak. I have become all things to all people, so that by all possible means I might save some.
I do all this for the sake of the good news, so that I may share in its blessings.
Don't you know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? So run in such a way as to win the prize.
Everyone who competes in the games trains hard in everything. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
So I do not run without a goal, and I do not fight like someone just hitting the air.
Instead, I treat my body hard and make it my slave, so that after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, I do not want you to forget that all our ancestors were under the cloud, and all of them passed through the sea.
They were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
They all ate the same spiritual food,
and they all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from the spiritual Rock that went with them, and that Rock was Christ.
But God was not pleased with most of them, and their bodies were scattered over the desert.
These things happened as examples for us, to keep us from setting our hearts on evil things as they did.
Do not worship idols, as some of them did. As the Scripture says: The people sat down to eat and drink and got up to play.
We should not commit sexual sin, as some of them did, and in one day twenty-three thousand of them died.
We should not test Christ, as some of them did, and were killed by snakes.
And do not complain, as some of them did, and were killed by the destroying angel.
These things happened to them as examples, and they were written down as warnings for us, on whom the end of the ages has come.
So if you think you are standing firm, be careful that you do not fall.

No temptation has come to you except what is common to everyone. And God is faithful. He will not let you be tempted beyond what you can handle. But when you are tempted, he will also give you a way out, so that you can stand up under it.
So, my dear friends, run away from the worship of idols.
I am speaking to you as sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
The cup of blessing that we give thanks for — is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? And the bread that we break — is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?
Because there is one loaf, we who are many are one body, for we all share the one loaf.
Think about the people of Israel: those who eat the sacrifices share in the altar, don't they?
So what am I saying? That an idol is anything, or that food offered to an idol is anything?
No, but the sacrifices of those who do not know God are offered to demons, not to God. And I do not want you to share with demons.
You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons too. You cannot share in the Lord's table and the table of demons.
Are we trying to make the Lord jealous? Are we stronger than he is?
“I am allowed to do anything” — but not everything is good. “I am allowed to do anything” — but not everything builds people up.
No one should look out only for his own good, but also for the good of others.
Eat anything that is sold in the meat market, without asking questions for the sake of your conscience,
for: The earth is the Lord's, and everything in it.
If someone who does not believe invites you to a meal and you want to go, eat whatever is put before you, without asking questions for the sake of conscience.
But if someone tells you, This food was offered in sacrifice, then do not eat it, for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience —
I mean his conscience, not yours. For why should my freedom be judged by someone else's conscience?
If I eat with thankfulness, why am I blamed for something I thank God for?
So whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do it all for the glory of God.
Do not cause anyone to stumble — not the Jews, not the other nations, and not the church of God.
This is what I do: I try to please everyone in every way. I am not looking out for my own good, but for the good of many, so that they may be saved.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ.
I praise you for remembering me in everything and for holding on to the teachings, just as I passed them on to you.
But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ, the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head.
And every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head. It is just as bad as if her head were shaved.
For if a woman does not cover her head, she might as well cut her hair off. But since it is a shame for a woman to cut off or shave her hair, she should cover her head.
A man should not cover his head, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is the glory of the man.
For man did not come from woman, but woman from man.
And man was not created for woman, but woman for man.
For this reason, and because of the angels, a woman should have a sign of authority on her head.
But in the Lord, woman is not separate from man, and man is not separate from woman.
For just as woman came from man, so man is born of woman. But everything comes from God.
Judge for yourselves: is it right for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
Does not nature itself teach you that it is a shame for a man to have long hair,
but that long hair is a woman's glory? For her hair is given to her as a covering.
If anyone wants to argue about this, we have no such practice, and neither do the churches of God.
Now in giving these instructions I cannot praise you, because your meetings do more harm than good.
First of all, I hear that when you come together as a church, there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
No doubt there have to be differences among you, to show which of you have God's approval.
When you come together, it is not really the Lord's Supper that you eat.
For as you eat, each of you goes ahead with his own supper without sharing. One person stays hungry, while another gets drunk.
Don't you have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I praise you? Not for this!
For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: that the Lord Jesus, on the night he was betrayed, took bread,
and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said: This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this to remember me.
In the same way, after supper he took the cup, saying: This cup is the new agreement in my blood. Whenever you drink it, do this to remember me.
For whenever you eat this bread and drink this cup, you are showing the Lord's death until he comes.
So whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in a way that is not worthy will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
So each person should examine himself before he eats the bread and drinks the cup.
For anyone who eats and drinks without recognizing the body of the Lord eats and drinks judgment on himself.
This is why many among you are weak and sick, and a number of you have died.
But if we judged ourselves truly, we would not be judged.
When the Lord judges us, he is correcting us, so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
So, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you meet together it will not bring judgment. And when I come, I will give further directions.
Now, brothers and sisters, I want you to understand about spiritual gifts.
Remember the lives you lived before you were believers. You let yourselves be influenced and led away to worship idols, things that have no life.

That is why I tell you: If the Spirit of God is in someone’s heart, that person can not curse Jesus. And when someone believes that Jesus is the Lord, he can say that only because the Holy Spirit is in his heart.
There are different gifts, but it is the same Spirit who gives them.
And there are different ways to serve the Lord, but we serve the same Lord.
And there are different kinds of ways that God works in the congregation, but it is the same God who helps people do all these things.
The Spirit shows everyone what he must do, because the Spirit knows what is best for the congregation.
The Spirit gives one person the ability to speak with wisdom. And the same Spirit gives another person the ability to speak with knowledge.
The same Spirit gives faith to one person and to another he gives gifts of healing.

The Spirit gives to one person the power to do miracles, to another the ability to prophesy, and to another the ability to judge what is from the Spirit and what is not. The Spirit gives one person the ability to speak in different kinds of languages, and to another the ability to interpret those languages.
One Spirit, the same Spirit, does all these things. The Spirit decides what to give each one.
Every person has a body, but his body has many parts. Yes, there are many parts, but the many parts are one body. It is the same with the body of Christ. It has many parts.
When we were baptized, the Holy Spirit made us all part of one body. We are Jews, Greeks, slaves and free people but the same Spirit is in all of us.
Our bodies do not have only one part. They have many parts.
If the foot says: I am not part of the body because I am not the hand, is the foot then not part of the body? No, it is still part of the body.
If the ear says: I am not part of the body because I am not the eye, is the ear then not part of the body? No, it is still part of the body.
If the whole body were an eye, then the body would not have been able to hear. And if the whole body were an ear, then the body would not have been able to smell.
But now God has given different parts to the body. He gave every part as He wanted.
If the whole body were just one part, then it would not have been a body.
But now there are many parts, and there is only one body.
The eye can not say to the hand: I do not need you. The head can not say to the feet: I do not need you.
We think there are parts of the body that are weaker than other parts, but the body needs them.
And the parts that we think are not worth very much are the parts we give the most care to. And we give special care to the parts of the body that we don’t want to show.
The more beautiful parts don’t need this special care. But God put the body together and gave more honor to the parts that need it.
That is why the body does not fight against itself, because the different parts of the body care for each other.
When one part of the body suffers, then all the other parts also suffer. And when the people praise one part of the body, then all the other parts are also happy.
All of you together are the body of Christ. You all are a part of this body

God gives each one of us a place in the church. First there are the apostles, 2nd are the prophets, 3rd are those who teach other people, then the people who do miracles, then those who have the gift of healing people and then those who help other people. There are people who can speak in tongues.
Not all of them are apostles, not all are prophets, not everyone can teach other people and not everyone can do miracles.
Not all have the gift of healing other people, not everyone speaks in tongues, and not everyone can interpret what people say when they speak in tongues.
Continue to give your attention to the spiritual gifts you consider to be the greatest. But now I want to point out a way of life that is even greater

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: If I speak in the languages of people and even of angels, but do not have love, I am only a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
If I have the gift of prophecy and understand all secrets and all knowledge, and if I have faith strong enough to move mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
If I give away everything I own, and even give my body to be burned, but do not have love, I gain nothing.
Love is patient and kind. Love is not jealous. It does not brag, and it is not proud.
It is not rude. It does not look out only for itself. It does not get angry easily, and it keeps no record of wrongs.
Love is not glad about evil, but is glad about the truth.
Love always protects, always trusts, always hopes, and always keeps going.
Love never ends. But prophecies will stop, tongues will be stilled, and knowledge will pass away.
For we know only a part, and we prophesy only a part.
But when what is perfect comes, what is only a part will pass away.
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I grew up, I put childish ways behind me.
For now we see only a dim picture, as in a poor mirror. But then we will see face to face. Now I know only a part; then I will know fully, just as I am fully known.
So these three remain: faith, hope, and love. But the greatest of these is love.

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Run after love, and also long for the gifts of the Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
For anyone who speaks in a tongue is not speaking to people but to God. No one understands him; he is speaking secrets by the Spirit.
But the one who prophesies speaks to people to build them up, encourage them, and comfort them.
The one who speaks in a tongue builds up himself, but the one who prophesies builds up the church.

I would like all of you to speak in tongues, but even more I would like you to prophesy. The one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in tongues, unless someone explains it, so that the church may be built up.
Now, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how does that help you? It helps only if I bring you some message, knowledge, prophecy, or teaching.
It is the same with lifeless things that make sounds, like a flute or a harp. Unless the notes are clear, how will anyone know what tune is being played?
And if the trumpet does not make a clear sound, who will get ready for battle?
It is the same with you. Unless you speak words that are easy to understand, how will anyone know what you are saying? You will just be speaking into the air.
There are surely many different languages in the world, and none of them is without meaning.
But if I do not understand the language, I will be a foreigner to the speaker, and he will be a foreigner to me.
It is the same with you. Since you are eager for the gifts of the Spirit, try to do well at building up the church.
So anyone who speaks in a tongue should pray that he can explain what he says.
For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind does not understand.
So what should I do? I will pray with my spirit, but I will also pray with my mind. I will sing with my spirit, but I will also sing with my mind.
If you praise God only with your spirit, how can someone who does not understand say “Amen” to your thanks, since he does not know what you are saying?
You may be giving thanks well enough, but the other person is not built up.
I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
But in the church I would rather speak five words that people understand, to teach others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
Brothers and sisters, do not be children in your thinking. Be like babies when it comes to evil, but be grown-ups in your thinking.
In the law it is written: With other languages and other lips I will speak to this people, but even then they will not listen to me, says the Lord.
So tongues are a sign, not for believers but for those who do not believe. But prophecy is for believers, not for those who do not believe.
So if the whole church comes together and everyone speaks in tongues, and people who do not understand or do not believe come in, will they not say that you are out of your minds?
But if everyone prophesies, and someone who does not believe or does not understand comes in, he will be shown his sin by all and judged by all.
The secrets of his heart will be brought to light. So he will fall down and worship God, saying: God is really among you!

So what should you do, brothers and sisters? When you come together, each of you has a song, a teaching, a tongue, a message from God, or an explanation. Everything must be done to build up the church.
If anyone speaks in a tongue, two — or at most three — should speak, one at a time, and someone should explain.
If there is no one to explain, the speaker should keep quiet in the church and speak only to himself and to God.
Two or three prophets should speak, and the others should weigh carefully what is said.
And if a message comes to someone sitting nearby, the first speaker should stop.
For you can all prophesy, one at a time, so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
The spirits of the prophets are under the control of the prophets.
For God is not a God of confusion, but of peace, as in all the churches of God's people.
The women should keep quiet in the churches. They are not allowed to speak, but should be submissive, as the law also says.
If they want to learn something, they should ask their own husbands at home, because it is a shame for a woman to speak in the church.
Did the word of God begin with you? Or are you the only people it has reached?
If anyone thinks he is a prophet or a spiritual person, he should recognize that what I am writing to you is the Lord's command.
But if anyone ignores this, he will be ignored.
So, my brothers and sisters, be eager to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.
But everything should be done in a proper and orderly way.
Now, brothers and sisters, I want you to remember the gospel I told you. You received that Good News, and you continue to base your life on it.
That Good News, the message you heard from me and believed, is God’s way to save you. Do not change the word which I preached to you, unless you believed in vain (without complete commitment].
I, Paul, gave you the message that I received. I told you the most important truths: that Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
Jesus was buried and Jesus was raised to life on the third day, as the Scriptures say;
Jesus appeared to Peter, then to all twelve apostles
Later Jesus appeared to more than 500 of his followers at once, most of whom are still alive, although some have died.
Then Jesus appeared to James and later to all the apostles.
Last of all, he appeared to me, Paul. I was different, like a baby born before the normal time.
All the other apostles are more important than I am. I am least worthy to be an apostle because I persecuted the people of the church of God.

Because of God’s grace, I am an apostle now. God’s grace that he gave me was not wasted. I worked harder than all the other apostles. (But I was not really the one working. It was God’s grace that was with me.)
It is not important who preaches to you, I or the other apostles. What is important is that we all preach the same message and that you believe it.

We tell everyone that Christ was raised from death. So why do some of you say that people will not be raised from death? 1 Corinthians 15: 13 If no one will ever be raised from death, then Christ has never been raised.
And if Christ has never been raised, then the message we tell is worth nothing. And your faith is worth nothing.

Then we have lied and we are false witnesses of God, because we said that God has raised Christ to live again. Because if the people who have died will not live again, then it is not true that God has raised Christ to live again. 1 Corinthians 15 16 If those who have died are not raised, then Christ has not been raised either.
And if God did not make Christ live again, then your faith is for nothing; you are still guilty of your sins
Then everyone who belonged to Christ and has died is lost.
If we hope that Christ saves us only for this life on earth, then everyone must feel very sorry for us.
But our message is true: God has raised Christ from death to live again, this is sure. Christ is the first One who died and was raised to live again.
We all die because of what one man, Adam, did. But all believers will be raised to live again because of what one Man, Christ, did.
We are all descendants of Adam. That is why we all die. But we also belong to Christ and that is why God will make us all live again.
But everyone will be raised to life in the right order. Christ was first to be raised. Then, when Christ comes again, those who belong to him will be raised to life.
Then the end will come. Christ will destroy all rulers, authorities, and powers. Then he will give the kingdom to God the Father
Christ will rule until God has defeated all enemies of Christ and put them under his feet.
The last enemy that Christ will defeat is death.

It is written in the Old Testament: God put everything under Christ control. When God says that everything is put under Christ, it is clear that this does not include God himself. God is the one putting everything under Christ’s control.

When God gives everything to Christ so that Christ can rule over it, then Christ, the Son of God, will also give Himself to God. Remember, God is the One who gave everything to Christ so that Christ can rule over it. That is why God will also rule over Christ in the end. Then God will rule over everything and everyone.
If no one will ever be raised from death, then what will the people do who are baptized for those who have died? If the dead are never raised, then why are people baptized for them?
30 And what about us? Why do we put ourselves in danger every hour if the dead will not live again?
I swear, dear brothers and sisters, that I face death daily, because as my pride in what Christ Jesus our Lord has done in you.
In Ephesus I fought wild animals. If I did that only for human reasons, then I have gained nothing. If we are not raised from death, Let us eat and drink, because tomorrow we die.
Do not let people deceive you, Bad friends will ruin good habits.
Come back to your right way of thinking and stop sinning. Some of you don’t know God. I say this to shame you.
Some people ask: What will it be like when the dead people live again? What will their bodies look like?
Why do they ask these foolish questions? When you plant something, it must die in the ground before it can live and grow.
And when you plant something, what you plant does not have the same body that it will have later. What you plant is only a seed, maybe wheat or something else
But God gives it the body that he has planned for it, and he gives each kind of seed its own body.
All things made of flesh are not the same: People have one kind of flesh, animals have another, birds have another, and fish have yet another kind.
Also there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. But the beauty of the heavenly bodies is one kind, and the beauty of the earthly bodies is another.
The sun has one kind of beauty, the moon has another kind, and the stars have another. And each star is different in its beauty.
It will be the same when those who have died are raised to life. The body that is buried in the grave will ruin and decay, but it will be raised to a life that cannot be destroyed.
When the body is buried it is without honor. But when it is raised, it will be great and glorious. When the body is buried, it is weak. But when it is raised, it will be full of power.
The body that is buried is a physical body. When it is raised, it will be a spiritual body. There is only a human body, there is also a spiritual body.
In the Old Testament it is written: The first man, Adam, became a living person. the last Adam, Christ, became a life-giving spirit to restoring the dead to life.
Remember, the spiritual body did not come first. The human body was first and after that, the spiritual body came.
God made the first man from the dust of the earth. But the 2nd Man is the Lord from heaven.

People who belong to this world are like the first man. They are like the man who came from. But people who belong to heaven are like the Man who came from heaven. Christ is the One who came from heaven.
Now we all are like the man of dust, but the time will come when we will be like the Man from heaven.
I tell you this, brothers and sisters: Our bodies of flesh and blood cannot have a part in God’s kingdom. Something that will ruin cannot have a part in something that never ruins.
Now I want to tell you a secret: Not all of us will die, but God will change us all.

It will only take the time of a second. We will be changed as quickly as an eye blink. This will happen when the last trumpet blows. The trumpet will blow and those who have died will be raised to live forever. And we will all be changed.
Because this body that we have now can become dust, God will give us a body that can not become dust. This body that we have now can die, but God will give us a body that will never die.
When God has given us a body that can not die or become dust, we will know that the words in the Old Testament are true where it is written: God has defeated death.
Death, where is your victory? Where is your power to hurt?
The power and sting of death come from sin, and the power of sin comes from the law.
But we thank God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
So, my dear brothers and sisters, stand strong. Don’t let anything change you. Always give yourselves fully to the work of the Lord. You know that your work in the Lord is never wasted.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now about the collection for God's people: do what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
On the first day of every week, each of you should set aside some money, in keeping with how much you have earned, and save it up. Then no collections will have to be made when I come.
When I arrive, I will give letters of introduction to the people you approve, and send them to take your gift to Jerusalem.
And if it seems best for me to go too, they will go with me.
I will come to you after I go through Macedonia, for I am going to pass through there.
Maybe I will stay with you for a while, or even spend the winter, so that you can help me on my journey, wherever I go.
I do not want to see you only in passing. I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord allows.
But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
because a great door for useful work has opened to me, even though there are many who oppose me.
When Timothy comes, make sure he has nothing to fear while he is with you, because he is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
So no one should look down on him. Send him on his way in peace, so that he can come back to me. I am expecting him, along with the other brothers.
Now about our brother Apollos: I strongly urged him to go to you with the others. But he did not want to go now; he will go when he has the chance.
Be on your guard. Stand firm in the faith. Be brave. Be strong.
Do everything in love.
You know that the household of Stephanas were the first to believe in Achaia, and they have given themselves to serving God's people. So I urge you, brothers and sisters,
to follow the lead of such people, and of everyone who joins in the work and serves.
I was glad when Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus arrived, because they have made up for your absence.
They have refreshed my spirit, and yours too. So show your appreciation to people like them.
The churches in Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Priscilla greet you warmly in the Lord, along with the church that meets in their house.
All the brothers and sisters here send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be cursed. Come, O Lord!
May the grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
